choiceresponse.label: Based on the structure of the tree, which statements are true?  (Select all that apply.)

checkboxgroup.choice: thal_2 affects predictions for all patients.
age influences predictions only for patients routed to the right subtree of the root.
chol influences predictions for all patients, but only weakly because it appears deep in the tree.
Features that appear deeper in the tree contribute less to predictions than features near the root.

div.p: Decision trees operate through conditional feature usage: a feature influences a prediction only if a patient reaches the node where that feature is tested. Because thal_2 appears at the root, it affects the routing of every patient. By contrast, age appears only in the right subtree, so it influences predictions only for that subset of patients. Features that appear deeper in the tree are not inherently weaker or less important; they may be highly informative for specific subgroups. Tree interpretability therefore comes from understanding which features are used for which patients, not from assuming a global ordering of importance based on depth.
